query_normalized: assessing the impact of data filtering on gravitational lens detection rates physics
rationale: This paper focuses on remote sensing object detection (e.g., planes, buildings in satellite imagery) using diffusion models, which is an off-domain application compared to the user's question about gravitational lens detection in astrophysics. It falls under the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords (e.g., "detection," "data filtering") without sharing the underlying physical mechanism or empirical population.
